resources.string: Smart Log Recorder with AI Analysis
Records system logs from your Android device and analyzes errors using Gemini AI.

1. First grant READ_LOGS permission (via Root or ADB).
2. Select a recording type from the main screen and start recording.
3. Exit the app (recording continues in background) and reproduce the issue a few times. (For example, try opening a crashing app three or four times)
4. Stop recording - the file is saved automatically.
5. Tap "Ask AI About the Error", select the log file you want to send, briefly describe your issue and send.
6. Apply the solution steps provided by AI.
A folder must be selected so log files can be saved to your device. The app only accesses the folder you choose.

While recording runs in the background, a status notification is shown in the notification bar. You can stop the recording from there. Without notification permission, the background service cannot run.
This app records system logs to help diagnose errors using AI analysis. Please select a folder to save your log files.
Errors and warnings only. READ_LOGS permission required.
Standard Logcat recording. READ_LOGS permission required.
All system and kernel logs. ROOT permission required.
A recording is already in progress
Deep Log records all system and kernel logs. This may take a while. Do you want to continue?
Visible while log recording is in progress
Briefly describe the problem you experienced...
No questions asked to AI yet
Select Recording File (Light logs only, Max 100 KB)
File size exceeds the 100 KB limit!
No log history found. Please record a log first.

KariD Log Manager records system activity on your Android device so you can see exactly what happened when an error occurred. App crashing? Phone freezing? Logcat recording captures the source of these issues and AI analysis suggests a solution.
Record system logs and use AI to diagnose Android errors quickly and accurately.
Different recording modes capture different levels of data.
Light recording: Records only error logs. File size is small and best suited for AI analysis.
Normal Log: Standard Logcat recording. Use this to monitor detailed app behavior.
Deep Log: Records all system and kernel logs, SELinux entries. Used for hardware or system-level issue detection. ROOT access required.

1. First grant READ_LOGS permission (via Root or ADB).
2. Select a recording type from the main screen and start recording.
3. Exit the app (recording continues in background) and reproduce the issue a few times. (For example, try opening a crashing app three or four times)
4. Stop recording - the file is saved automatically.
5. Tap "Ask AI About the Error", select the log file you want to send, briefly describe your issue and send.
6. Apply the solution steps provided by AI.
All AI history will be deleted. Are you sure?
READ_LOGS permission is required to read system and app logs. Choose one of the methods below.
Could not grant via root. Try the ADB method.
1. Install Android Debug Bridge (ADB) on your computer.\n2. Connect your phone via USB with USB Debugging enabled.\n3. Run the following command:
This permission persists until the app is uninstalled or factory reset. Only needs to be done once.
Permission not yet granted. Run the command and try again.
READ_LOGS permission missing. Only this apps logs will be recorded.
READ_LOGS permission is not granted. Only this apps own logs will be recorded. Do you want to grant the permission first?

READ_LOGS permission is required to read logs from other apps on Android 4.1+. Recording is not possible without this permission.

Method 1 - Root (Recommended): Grant root access via Magisk/KernelSU. Permission is obtained automatically. Logcat runs as root for maximum stability.

Method 2 - ADB: Connect your phone to a computer with USB Debugging enabled and run the permission command. Works on all devices, valid until the app is uninstalled.

Note: On official Android 12+ ROMs, READ_LOGS is restricted to system apps only. However, some custom ROMs like CrDroid and LineageOS grant this permission to third-party apps without requiring ADB. On these ROMs you can use the app as if permission was granted via ADB, without needing ROOT access.

KariD Log Manager requires READ_LOGS permission to record logs.

• Method 1 - Root (Recommended): Grant root access via your root manager (Magisk/KernelSU). Permission is obtained automatically.
• Method 2 - ADB: One-time command via computer. Works on all devices.

See the "Log Read Permission Required" screen for details.
Install ADB on your computer. Enable USB Debugging on your phone and connect it. Run the following command in ADB:
READ_LOGS permission is required to start recording. Please grant the permission first.
Grant root access to KariD Log Manager via your root manager (Magisk/KernelSU etc.). The app will obtain READ_LOGS permission automatically and all log types will work reliably.
READ_LOGS permission is sufficient for Light and Normal recording. ROOT access is required for the SELinux and Kernel logs captured in Deep recording.
1 - Grant ROOT access to the app to provide READ_LOGS permission.
2 - Grant READ_LOGS permission to the app via ADB.

* Device info is written at the top of every log file.
* Maximum log size that can be sent to AI is 100 KB.
* Only light log recordings can be sent to AI. For issues you could not solve with light recording, record with Normal or Deep and analyze with your own AI apps.
* Share Deep log files only with trusted people - DEEP LOG FILES MAY CONTAIN PERSONAL INFORMATION!
Which log levels are recorded for each recording type?

On some Android 13+ devices, the system may show you a permission dialog. Tap the button below and select "Allow" in the dialog that appears. NOTE: This method only works on certain devices (custom ROMs like CrDroid, LineageOS). Stock Android and Google Pixel devices do not grant this permission to third-party apps.
READ_LOGS permission granted automatically via Root.
This method may not be supported on your device. If permission was not granted, use the Root or ADB method.
WARNING: If you received permission via this method, do not leave the app while recording. On some devices, background recording access may be cut off.
Root permission: If you grant root access (Magisk, KernelSU etc.), READ_LOGS is obtained automatically and works reliably for all recording types. This is the most reliable method for rooted users.
⚠ Only works on some ROMs (CrDroid, LineageOS etc.). Not supported on stock Android or Pixel.
Method 1 - Root (Recommended): If you root your device and grant root access to this app, READ_LOGS permission is obtained automatically. This is the easiest and most reliable method.
Your device is not rooted. Root method is unavailable. Please use the ADB method.

Note: On Android 12+ stock ROMs, READ_LOGS is restricted to system apps only. However, some custom ROMs like CrDroid and LineageOS grant this permission without requiring ADB. On these ROMs you can use the app without ROOT access, as if permission was granted via ADB. Root access is still the recommended method.
On Android 4.1 and above, READ_LOGS permission is required to read logs from other apps. Without this permission, only the app\'s own logs are recorded.

1. Magisk or KernelSU must be installed on your device.
2. Open KariD Log Manager.
3. In your root manager (Magisk/KernelSU), find KariD Log Manager and grant root access.
4. The app automatically obtains READ_LOGS permission. Logcat runs as root during recording.

1. Download ADB:
 https://developer.android.com/studio/releases/platform-tools
 Extract "platform-tools.zip" to a folder (e.g. C:\platform-tools).
2. Enable USB Debugging on your phone:
 Settings > About Phone > Tap Build Number 7 times
 Then: Settings > Developer Options > Enable USB Debugging.
3. Connect phone via USB and approve the connection on your phone.
4. Open a command prompt:
 In the C:\platform-tools folder, type cmd in the address bar and press Enter.
5. Type and run the following command:
adb shell pm grant com.karid.logmanager android.permission.READ_LOGS

Note: On stock Android 12+ ROMs, READ_LOGS is restricted to system apps only. Some custom ROMs like CrDroid and LineageOS leave this permission open to third-party apps without requiring ADB. On these ROMs the permission works automatically. Root access is still the recommended method.
For detailed instructions, see the Info (ℹ) section.
If the command runs without error, permission has been granted. This permission remains valid until the app is uninstalled or the phone is factory reset.
